questions.q: Which of the following represents a native Kubernetes resource used to authenticate an application or workload running in the cluster?
questions.explanation: ServiceAccounts are native Kubernetes objects created inside the cluster to represent workloads and applications. Kubernetes does not have a native User resource; human users are managed externally.

questions.q: Kubernetes natively stores human user credentials like passwords inside its etcd database.
questions.explanation: False. Kubernetes does not manage or store human user accounts or credentials natively in etcd. It relies on external mechanisms such as Client Certificates, OIDC, or external LDAP directories.
